mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units starts above 1.1 million signals a strengthening demand for housing that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1100000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5% signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE < 0.055 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 99 signals a strengthening supply chain that boosts productivity and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 99 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 1.3 trillion signals a strengthening demand for consumer goods that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 1300000000000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment above 135 million signals a strengthening labor market that boosts wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS > 135000000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 2.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 0.025 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 1375 billion signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 1375000000000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
